questions.question: Which of the following creatures did God command into existence on the second day of creation?
questions.explanation: On the second day of creation, God created the firmament (the sky/expanse) to divide the waters above from the waters below.

questions.question: The determination of times and seasons was made possible with the creation of?
questions.explanation: God created the stars along with the sun and moon to mark times, seasons, days, and years for humanity and all creation.

questions.question: Which of the following was man's responsibility to other creatures?
questions.explanation: God gave man dominion over all creatures, meaning he was to exercise authority and stewardship over them.

questions.question: In which of the following ways did God prepare Joseph for his leadership role in Egypt? Through the

options.text: love his brothers had for him

questions.explanation: God prepared Joseph through his tribulations—his slavery, false accusation, and imprisonment—which developed his character and faith.

questions.question: Which of the following was the greatest reason for which Joseph's brothers decided to do away with him?

options.text: his father made him a special robe
he was loved most by their father

questions.explanation: Joseph's brothers were threatened by the meaning of his dreams, which suggested he would rule over them, motivating their jealousy and hatred.

questions.question: The twelve men Moses sent to spy out the land of Canaan were meant to verify the following except the

options.text: strength and weakness of the inhabitants
population and size of the land
size of the army and their weaponery
topography and fertility of the land

questions.explanation: The spies were to assess the land's geography, fertility, inhabitants' strength, and size, but not specifically to report on the enemy's army size and weapons.

questions.question: The presence of God among His people during the day on their way out of Egypt to the wilderness of Sinai was felt in the form of
questions.explanation: God's presence appeared as a pillar of cloud by day and a pillar of fire by night to guide and protect the Israelites during their wilderness journey.

sections.sectionName: Old Testament - Joshua and the Judges

questions.question: Which of the following was God's condition to Joshua if he wishes to be successful? He should

options.text: meditate on the book of the law

questions.explanation: God commanded Joshua to meditate on the book of the law day and night, keeping it in his mouth so he could observe all written in it for his success.

questions.question: When the people of Israel heard the majority report of the spies, they took a decision to

options.text: pray to God to come to their aid
choose a leader and return to egypt
stone joshua and caleb for blasphemy

questions.explanation: Discouraged by the spies' negative report, the Israelites rebelled against God and decided to appoint a leader to take them back to Egypt.

questions.question: The history of Barak's war, story flawed by the fact he

options.text: consulted the oracles to find out the outcome of his battle
conspired with Jael to take out Sisera and Canaanites
took the tribes of Zebulun and Naphtali to war with him
sought the presence of Deborah as a reinforcement to his courage

questions.explanation: Barak's faith was flawed because he relied on Deborah's presence for courage rather than fully trusting God's promise to lead him to victory.

questions.question: After whose death did God sell the Israelites into the hands of king Jabin?
questions.explanation: After Ehud's death, the Israelites returned to sin, and God sold them into the hands of Jabin, king of Canaan, for twenty years.

questions.question: Which of the following led to the death of Eli?

options.text: it was his time to die

questions.explanation: Eli died when he heard news of the capture of the ark of God and the death of his sons. The shock caused him to fall backward and break his neck.

questions.question: Yet his sons did not walk in his ways but turned aside for gains, they took bribes ... The sons being referred to here were
questions.explanation: Hophni and Phinehas, Eli's sons, became corrupt priests who took bribes and did not follow their father's righteous ways.

questions.question: The account of Saul's rejection as a king is intended to

options.text: explain the breach between samuel and saul
show how unfit saul was as a leader
show the desire for power by men of God

questions.explanation: Saul's rejection teaches the important lesson that disobedience to God's commands, regardless of status or authority, leads to downfall.

questions.question: Rather than killing Saul, David took his spear and jar of water and rebuked Abner for
questions.explanation: David rebuked Abner for negligence and dereliction of duty in failing to properly guard King Saul while he slept.

questions.question: Saul's death on Mount Gilboa was as a consequence of his

options.text: armor bearer's obedience to his command
loss of confidence in David the musician
sorcery by consulting the witch of Endor

questions.explanation: Saul's death was the ultimate consequence of his persistent disobedience to God's commands throughout his reign as king.

sections.sectionName: Old Testament - Solomon and the Prophets

questions.question: Solomon enjoyed the following gifts during his reign as King except
questions.explanation: While Solomon enjoyed wisdom, wealth, fame, and many wives, his succession was not particularly peaceful due to the division of the kingdom after his death.

questions.question: God registered His displeasure with David for bearing a child with Uriah's wife by striking the child with
questions.explanation: God struck the child born of David and Bathsheba with sickness, and the child died as punishment for David's sin of adultery and murder.

questions.question: Solomon assembled the elders and leaders of the tribes of Israel in order to

options.text: discuss how to build the temple
bring back the ark of the Lord

questions.explanation: Solomon assembled the leaders of Israel to bring back the ark of the Lord from the City of David to its place in the temple he had built.

questions.question: Which of the following punishment was meted out to Solomon as a result of his idolatry?

options.text: He would lose all his wives and concubines to his enemies
All the tribes of Israel would be taken away from him
All his children and wives would go into exile
All the palaces and store houses would be occupied by his enemies

questions.explanation: Due to Solomon's idolatry and turning from God, he was punished with the loss of the kingdom—most of the tribes were torn away from his descendants.

questions.question: Obadiah demonstrated his loyalty to God during Ahab's reign where he

options.text: refused to serve the king's idols
hid the prophets of God in caves
tore down the altars of Baal

questions.explanation: Obadiah demonstrated loyalty to God by hiding one hundred prophets in caves and feeding them bread and water when Ahab sought to kill them.

questions.question: The miraculous healing of Naaman teaches mankind that

options.text: river Jordan is full of healing powers
God is not limited to Israel

questions.explanation: Naaman's healing demonstrates that God's healing power and mercy extend to all people, regardless of nationality or status.

questions.question: Which of the following reasons cause Naboth not to part with his vineyard?
questions.explanation: Naboth refused to sell his vineyard because it was a family inheritance passed down from his ancestors, which was sacred in Israelite law.

questions.question: King Josiah took a courageous step in the presence of the Israelites by the pillar when he

options.text: made a covenant before the Lord

questions.explanation: Josiah made a covenant before the Lord in the presence of the people, pledging to follow God and keep His commandments.

questions.question: The immediate action King Josiah took during his religious reforms was the

options.text: breaking down of the house of the male-cult prostitutes
removal of all idolatrous priests from offices
removal and burning of the vessels of Baal and Asherah
defiling of the high places where the priests burnt incense

questions.explanation: Josiah's first action in religious reform was removing and burning the vessels of Baal and Asherah from the temple to purge idolatry.

questions.question: In which of the following ways was Nehemiah said to have displayed patriotism? When he

options.text: asked permission to visit his home
collected taxes from the Jews for the home country
felt the plight of his people and took remedial steps

questions.explanation: Nehemiah displayed patriotism by feeling compassion for his people's distress and taking action to rebuild the walls of Jerusalem.

questions.question: What are these feeble Jews doing? These words of Sanballat teach us that

options.text: no matter what we do, people will ridicule us
we must be vigilant while we work
people would appreciate our weakness and assist

questions.explanation: Sanballat's mocking words teach that opposition and ridicule can come regardless of the righteousness of one's work or effort.

questions.question: That Daniel, who is one of the exiles from Judah, pays no heed to you, O king ... The king being addressed here was
questions.explanation: This refers to King Darius of Persia, who issued the decree that Daniel should not pray to anyone but the king, and Daniel refused to obey.

questions.question: Seek me and live; but do not seek Bethel and do not enter Gilgah ... This text from Amos which was a reaction to the mode of worship of the Israelites express the

options.text: futility of false and cultic worship

questions.explanation: Amos condemns the Israelites' reliance on cultic worship at Bethel and Gilgal, declaring it futile; instead, they must seek God himself.

questions.question: The saying that the children of Israel shall dwell many days without ephod means

options.text: complete starvation in Israel is inevitable
no priestly activities will be in Israel
Israel will be exposed to calamity

questions.explanation: The ephod was worn by the priest; Israel being without it means there will be no priestly or religious activities—a spiritual desolation.

questions.question: Immediately after Jesus was baptized, the following incidents occurred except

options.text: angels came and ministered to him
the Spirit of God descended on him like a dove
a voice from heaven introduced him as son of God

questions.explanation: While the heavens opened, the Spirit descended as a dove, and a voice confirmed Jesus as God's Son, angels ministering to Him occurred after His temptation, not immediately after baptism.

questions.question: Which of the following attributes of Jesus was demonstrated during his temptation?

options.text: reliance on the Holy Spirit in him

questions.explanation: During His temptation, Jesus demonstrated His reliance on the Holy Spirit and God's word, refusing to use His divine power for selfish purposes.

questions.question: Whoever does not bear his own cross and come after me, cannot be my disciple. This statement implies?
questions.explanation: Bearing one's cross means denying oneself, submitting to God's will, and being willing to sacrifice personal desires for following Christ.

questions.question: Jesus' call of Matthew, the tax collector, is a demonstration that Christians

options.text: are free to call anyone they like
need to study people closely before calling them
must be careful with people of character

questions.explanation: Jesus' calling of Matthew demonstrates that God's grace extends to all people, including sinners, without discrimination or judgment.

questions.question: Which of the following virtues enhances forgiveness?
questions.explanation: Mercy—compassion and compassionate treatment of others—is the virtue that most directly enhances and enables forgiveness.

questions.question: All the following characteristics were true of the early church except

options.text: complaints about unfair distribution of food
admission into the church through baptism
facing persecution boldly after Stephen's death

questions.explanation: While the early church did share possessions, had distribution issues, and required baptism for admission, many believers fled or were scattered due to persecution after Stephen's death rather than facing it boldly together.

questions.question: The arrival of the Holy Spirit on the day of pentecost was experienced by the disciples through
questions.explanation: On the day of Pentecost, the disciples experienced the Holy Spirit through a mighty rushing wind and tongues of fire appearing over their heads.

questions.question: Which of the following incidents occurred following Peter's post-pentecost sermon which led to the baptism of three thousand souls? The converts

options.text: Devoted themselves to the apostles teaching and fellowship
created disciplinary problems for the apostles
occasioned the appointment of the deacons
contributed to the welfare of the members

questions.explanation: After three thousand were baptized, the converts devoted themselves to the apostles' teaching, fellowship, breaking bread, and prayer.

questions.question: One of the qualifications set out by the apostles as a requirement for the appointment of the deacons was
questions.explanation: The apostles required deacons to be of good standing, full of the Spirit and wisdom, and of moral uprightness to serve the widows and needy.

questions.question: One of the outcomes of the persecutions of the early church was that

options.text: believers left Jerusalem to settle in other towns
fear gripped the followers into utter silence
the enrollment of the church community dwindled
those who were scattered carried out evangelism

questions.explanation: The persecution that followed Stephen's death caused believers to scatter, but those scattered preached the gospel, spreading Christianity to new areas.

questions.question: Jesus told the story of the servant who owed his master in order to teach
questions.explanation: The parable of the unforgiving servant teaches the importance of forgiveness, showing how we must forgive others as God forgives us.

questions.question: For which of the following reasons was the thief at the right hand of Jesus promised paradise? He

options.text: was not among those who crucified Jesus

questions.explanation: The thief on the cross was promised paradise because he acknowledged Jesus as Lord, confessed his sins, and showed genuine repentance.

questions.question: Eli Eli lama sabachthani. This citation by Jesus on the cross was construed to mean
questions.explanation: Eli Eli lama sabachthani means 'My God, My God, why have you forsaken me?'—Jesus' cry of anguish to God expressing the pain of the crucifixion.

sections.sectionName: New Testament - James, Peter and Christian Living

questions.question: According to James, unanswered prayers are caused by the following factors except
questions.explanation: James teaches that prayers go unanswered due to lack of faith, selfish motives, and praying outside God's will. Persistence in prayer is encouraged, not a cause of unanswered prayers.

questions.question: Religion that is pure and undefiled before God demands

options.text: keeping oneself unstained from the world
accepting Jesus as your Lord and saviour

questions.explanation: Pure and undefiled religion before God, according to James, is to care for the orphans and widows and to keep oneself unstained from the world.

questions.question: Which of the following, according to Peter, is a characteristic of a good citizen? One who

options.text: builds houses and rents to those who do not have
provides transport at reduced cost for other citizens
obeys the law of the state and fulfills his obligation
helps to finance the government all the time

questions.explanation: Peter teaches that a good citizen is one who obeys the laws of the state, respects those in authority, and fulfills their civic obligations.

questions.question: In the teaching of Peter, when Christians become good citizens, they

options.text: receive the crown of glory in heaven
silence the ignorance of foolish men
overcome the evil intentions of men

questions.explanation: Peter teaches that when Christians live as good citizens, doing right and obeying laws, they silence the ignorance of foolish people who oppose them.

questions.question: For christians to continue their privileged freedom Peter suggests that they should

options.text: use it as a pretext for evil

questions.explanation: Peter teaches that Christians should guard their freedom by living as servants of God and doing what is right, not using freedom as a pretext for evil.

questions.question: The concept of grace in Christian theology primarily refers to
questions.explanation: Grace is God's free, unmerited favor and blessing given to humanity. It is not earned through works but freely given by God's compassion.

questions.question: The second coming of Christ is characterized by all the following except
questions.explanation: The second coming involves judgment, resurrection, and God's eternal kingdom, but not immediate enjoyment of physical pleasures as some wrongly teach.
